findings.title: logs rejects supported IPv6 worker addresses
findings.impact: Existing CLI address validation accepts bracketed IPv6 worker addresses such as [::1]:31501, and the public API docs describe worker addresses as IPv4 or IPv6. This new validator only accepts a single host token followed by one colon, so `dscli logs -w [::1]:31501` fails before process lookup even when the worker is running and `dscli stop` can target the same address.
findings.fix: Reuse the existing `yr.datasystem.cli.common.util.is_valid_address_port` helper, or implement equivalent IPv4/bracketed-IPv6 host:port parsing, before escaping the address for `pgrep`.
